title: Will the 21M BTC cap create zero-sum wealth and thinking? (Jan 6 2022 — title-only macro question)
html:
TL;DR

  • Jan 6, 2022 note that exists entirely as its title: "Will the 21 million BTC cap create zero sum wealth and thinking." Body is empty.
  • One of the earliest dated crypto thinking artifacts in Yahir's vault — sitting in the freshman-winter window, ten months before the Ahmad Jamal junk drawer crystallizes the founder/Web3 stance.
  • The question itself is unusually sharp for a 19-year-old: does a hard-capped monetary asset structurally produce zero-sum wealth dynamics, and does that bleed into the mental models of the people who hold it? That's the value investing / MMT / scarcity-economics neighborhood compressed into one line.
  • The open question echoes the "predict the future" maxim from 9 months later — same pattern of capturing a thesis-shaped question without writing the answer.
  • Pairs naturally with the web3 starter pack (saved March 8) — Yahir's January-2022 question gets partial reading-list answers two months later.

Key quote

"Will the 21 million BTC cap create zero sum wealth and thinking"

Why it matters

This note matters because of what it isn't: it isn't a take, it's the question that would generate a take if Yahir had sat down to write one. Read alongside asking the right question and the value investing page, the Jan 2022 BTC-cap question is the kind of thing Yahir was capable of asking nine months before he started repeating the Munger / Garcia Martinez aphorisms back to himself. He was already operating at thesis-question level; what changed by late 2022 was the volume of capture, not the quality of the underlying inquiry.

Open questions

  • Did Yahir reach a position on this? (Best place to look: the web3 starter pack note + later 2023 crypto notes.)
  • Is "zero sum thinking" Yahir worried about adoption (people behaving like there's a fixed pie) or valuation (the math of a capped supply)?
  • This idea — capped-supply asset → zero-sum mental model — would slot cleanly into a MMT / UBI essay if Yahir ever wrote one.
